DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW YATES' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. No opposition filed. Defendant's demurrer to the Negligence and NIED causes of action are overruled. Defendant failed to address Plaintiff's claim that the HOA Bylaws impose a duty on Defendant not to create a nuisance or to interfere with the quiet enjoyment of another resident of the HOA or building. Defendant's demurrer to the IIED claims is overruled. Plaintiff alleges Defendant made false claims to Plaintiff's landlord that Plaintiff was using drugs and intentionally starting fires, all with the intent to drive Plaintiff out of her tenancy. Taking the allegations as true, a reasonable jury could find this type conduct constitutes outrageous behavior. Defendant's demurrer to the 11th cause of action for Tenant Harassment is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's allegations do not support an agency relationship, and the Court is not persuaded that Plaintiff can allege one based on the allegations in the TAC.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS PATRICIA HSIEH AND YOUFONG HSIEH'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) as to each element of this cause of action as to each defendant.
Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment - tort is overruled. Moving party acknowledges that tort damages are available for breach of this covenant under certain circumstances and does not cite any authorities for the proposition that recovery under the circumstances of this case is barred.
Demurrer to the cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress is overruled. Moving party does not argue that no cause of action is alleged and appears to take issue with the label. Allegations within this cause of action appear to be no more that negligence claim with request for damages; a claim Defendant asserts is proper under Molien v. Kaiser (1980) 27 Cal.3d 916. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
